title: Per-deliverable license, provenance, and not-medical-advice check procedure
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual requires a per-deliverable license and provenance check to ensure every entry is sourced from an allow-listed, permitted source with correct attribution and a not-medical-advice notice. This procedure formalizes the gate used informally in M0/M1 and provides the specification for automated enforcement in license-002 (M2). Depends on sources-001 (allow-list) and schema-001 (content schemas).
objective: Document a checkable per-deliverable procedure that verifies, for each glossary entry and translation: (a) source is allow-listed and derivative permission confirmed, (b) required attribution is present, (c) provenance is complete, (d) not-medical-advice notice is present, and (e) output license is source-compatible. Structure it as a checklist that tooling can later enforce.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/license-provenance-check.md defines a per-deliverable checklist verifying: source in allow-list with derivativesAllowed:true, required attribution string present verbatim, provenance fields complete (URL, version/date, retrieval date, snapshotHash), notMedicalAdvice:true, output license source-compatible
Procedure specifies what happens when a check fails: entry is held back (not delivered/done) and the source/attribution/provenance gap is recorded and resolved before resubmission
Checklist is structured so that a future automated tool (license-002) can implement it programmatically
Procedure covers both source-language entries and translations (translation inherits source attribution and notice)
resources: PLAN.md — Data, licensing and compliance section
output: docs/license-provenance-check.md with the per-deliverable checklist procedure
